Still Think You Need a Big
Following to Get Paid?

Influencer myths

Still think you need a giant following to make money from content?

You don't.

But it's not your fault for thinking that. Because for years, creators have been told the same story:

1 To work with brands, you have to grow a big audience.
2 To get paid, you have to go viral.
3 To “make it,” you have to become an influencer.
You've probably heard it all:
1 “You need 10K+ followers to be taken seriously.”
2 “You have to post every day to stay relevant.”
3 “You need a defined niche before you even think about pitching.”
Creator reality

But here's the truth most of the industry won't tell you:

It's all outdated.

The game has changed, and most creators are still playing by the old rules. Because the truth is, you don't need influence.

You need a value offer.

And today, creators are finally getting paid for what they actually create, not just what they can promote.

We're in a new era now.

One where brands care less about clout, and way more about conversion.

Followers ≠ income
Performance > popularity
Content = asset

New Horizon Creators · 03
Proof

Real Creator. Real Results.

And that's exactly what this guide is here to help you build.

Our student Nicolai landed 5 paid brand deals with less than 2K followers, and consistently makes between $3–5,000 per month.

NOW, let's bust the 4 biggest myths in getting paid brand deals, without being an influencer.
New Horizon Creators · 04
Myth #1 “I need a big audience to get paid.”
TRUTH
Brands don't pay for followers. They pay for results. We say this constantly, because it still surprises creators. Brands are not paying to “borrow your audience.”
They're paying for content that makes people:
Stop scrolling
Click
Buy
Remember their brand
That means your value isn't in your follower count. It's in your creative performance, your hooks, your storytelling, your ability to make a product feel real and needed by the audience.
Example
Our student Keanna landed her first brand deal with just 600 followers. What did the brand care about? Her sample video had a great hook, looked native to TikTok, and made the product feel essential, not promotional.
Community · First deal
New Horizon Creators · 05
Myth #2 “I have to post it on my own account.”
TRUTH

You're not promoting, you're producing.

This is the biggest mindset shift:

You're not a billboard. You're a content creator.

That means your content lives on the brand's channels, not yours. This is what we call UGC, user-generated content. And it comes with serious benefits:

You don't need to “perform” for your own audience.
You don't have to worry about your feed, grid, or aesthetic.
You don't have to play the algorithm game.
You can be as public or private as you want.
Here's why this matters, most creators burn out trying to grow their own page, when in reality, the money is in creating for brands, not posting about them.
New Horizon Creators · 06
Myth #3 “Brands only care about influencers.”
TRUTH
Brands care about performance, not popularity. Here's what brands are actually looking for:
Videos that get high watch time.
Ads that lower cost-per-click.
Content that performs in retargeting campaigns.
Creators who can test and iterate fast

You're not here to be liked, you're here to drive results.

And once a brand sees you as a growth tool, not just a content vendor, you go from being a one-off creator to a long-term partner.

Here's why this matters, you're not an “influencer.” You're a performance-based creator.

You don't need brand deals, you need brand partnerships.

Tell that to our student Kayleigh who landed over 3+ retainers with a tiny audience.

Community · Retainers
New Horizon Creators · 07
Myth #4 “I need experience to pitch.”
TRUTH
You don't need a big portfolio, you just need proof of concept.
Here's how you can get that in 48 hours or less:
1
Study real ads. Go to TikTok Creative Center and check the “Top Ads” in your niche.
2
Recreate a few. Film 2–3 sample videos in your style (voiceover, faceless, or on-cam).
3
Package them together. Create a clean 3-video sample folder (Google Drive is fine).
4
Start pitching. Brands don't need perfection. They need to see potential.

Brands don't expect you to be famous. They just want to know: “Can this person make content that looks like what we already pay for?”

You're not guessing. You're presenting proof.

Study adsRecreate samplesPackage + pitch
